sections.title: Market Pulse: Tech-Led Rally Continues Amid Geopolitical Easing

items.headline: U.S. Stocks Climb, Led by Technology, as Investors Reassess Risk
items.summary: U.S. equity markets closed higher as investors re-engaged with risk assets, particularly in the technology sector, driven by a perceived stabilization of geopolitical tensions in the Middle East. Record capital inflows into American stock funds, totaling $119.2 billion in a single week, underscore the robust market sentiment.

items.headline: Cerebras Systems Surges on Strong Performance
items.summary: Cerebras Systems, Inc. was among the day's top gainers.

items.headline: Micron Technology Jumps Ahead of Earnings
items.summary: Micron Technology, Inc. saw a significant rise, closing at $1133.99, ahead of its Q3 earnings report due after market close. The company had previously reported gross margins topping 68%, driven by strong demand in AI servers and data centers.

items.headline: STMicroelectronics Leads Semiconductor Gains
items.summary: STMicroelectronics N.V. posted strong gains as the semiconductor sector continued to lead technology shares higher.

items.headline: Work Medical Technology Group Plummets
items.summary: Work Medical Technology Group Ltd experienced a sharp decline in trading today.

items.headline: Veraxa Biotech Ag Sees Significant Drop
items.summary: Veraxa Biotech Ag shares fell substantially, making it one of the biggest decliners of the day.

items.headline: Accenture Shares Slide Amid Consulting Concerns
items.summary: Accenture Plc recorded its worst day in years, with shares falling significantly amidst broader market questions about the impact of AI on the consulting business.

items.headline: Federal Reserve Holds Rates Steady, Signals Future Hikes
items.summary: The Federal Reserve, under newly appointed Chair Kevin Warsh, maintained the federal funds rate target range at 3.5% to 3.75%. However, the FOMC delivered a hawkish surprise, with nine of 18 participants now forecasting at least one rate hike by the end of 2026, indicating potential further increases if inflation remains persistent. This stance has influenced borrowing costs and Treasury yields.

items.headline: US-Iran Peace Deal Boosts Sentiment, But Doubts Emerge
items.summary: A tentative peace deal between the U.S. and Iran initially provided a significant boost to market sentiment, contributing to lower oil prices, with Brent crude falling 9.5% to $79 per barrel. However, late-week clashes and Iran's announcement of closing the Strait of Hormuz again (though disputed by the U.S. military) have introduced renewed uncertainty about the agreement's durability and future oil supply.

items.headline: SpaceX Makes Historic Public Debut
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX completed its public debut, raising an estimated $85.7 billion in the largest IPO in history, valuing the company above $2 trillion. This monumental event has significantly shifted investor sentiment towards large technology and innovation-linked companies, despite some weekly decline.

items.headline: Technology Sector Continues to Lead Market Gains
items.summary: The technology sector, particularly chipmakers and AI-related themes, demonstrated strong leadership in the market, attracting $19.2 billion in investments. This bullish rotation saw the S&P 500 ex-Mag-7 reaching new all-time highs, while sectors like energy, financials, and communications lagged.

items.headline: Nvidia Plans $20 Billion Bond Issuance
items.summary: Nvidia Corporation ($NVDA), a key player in AI chips and data centers, announced plans to issue at least $20 billion in investment-grade bonds for general corporate purposes, marking one of its most substantial debt moves since the onset of the AI boom.
